questions.question: State the charge on the sulfate ion.
Write the formulae of (i) iron(II) chloride and (ii) iron(III) sulfate, showing clearly how the charges of the ions determine each formula.

Explain how the formula of aluminium nitrate, Al(NO₃)₃, is derived from the charges of the aluminium and nitrate ions. In your answer, state the charge on each ion and show that the overall charge of the compound is zero.
State the systematic name of the compound with the formula MnO₂, given that the oxide ion has a charge of 2−.
Deduce the correct formula of calcium phosphate from the following: (A) CaPO₄; (B) Ca₂(PO₄)₃; (C) Ca₃(PO₄)₂; (D) Ca₃PO₄. Show your reasoning using ion charges.
Describe how oxidation numbers are used to name compounds containing transition metals. Apply this to name (i) CrCl₂, (ii) CrCl₃, and (iii) Cu₂O. Show oxidation number working for each.
State the charge on the ammonium ion.
Write the formulae of (i) ammonium sulfate and (ii) ammonium phosphate.
A student writes the formula of magnesium hydroxide as MgOH₂. Explain why this is incorrect, write the correct formula, and state the rule about brackets that has been violated.
State the charge on the carbonate ion.
Deduce the formula of chromium(III) oxide and chromium(VI) oxide. Show your working using oxidation numbers.
Describe the 'cross-charge' method for deriving the formula of an ionic compound. Use it to derive the formula of tin(IV) chloride (SnCl₄), checking that the overall charge is zero.
State the name and formula of the anion present in potassium nitrate (KNO₃).
Write the formulae of barium chloride and barium carbonate, using the charges of the barium ion (Ba²⁺), chloride ion (Cl⁻), and carbonate ion (CO₃²⁻).

Explain why iron can form two different chloride compounds, iron(II) chloride (FeCl₂) and iron(III) chloride (FeCl₃). In your answer, state the oxidation number of iron in each compound and explain how you deduced the formula from the ion charges.
